s-1.ptxt: DISCHARGE FUEL SYSTEM PRESSURE
DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL
REMOVE REAR SEAT CUSHION ASSEMBLY (for Fixed Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Detach the 2 front hooks of the seat cushion from the vehicle body.

atten3.ptxt: Follow the instructions below carefully as the cushion frame can be deformed easily.

s3.ptxt: Choose a hook to detach first. Place your hands near the hook as shown in the illustration. Then lift the seat cushion to detach the hook.
Repeat the step above for the other hook.

s2.ptxt: Remove the rear seat cushion assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SEAT CUSHION ASSEMBLY (for Fold Down Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Detach the 2 front hooks of the seat cushion from the vehicle body.

atten3.ptxt: Follow the instructions below carefully as the cushion frame can be deformed easily.

s3.ptxt: Choose a hook to detach first. Place your hands near the hook as shown in the illustration. Then lift the seat cushion to detach the hook.
Repeat the step above for the other hook.

s2.ptxt: Remove the seat cushion.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SEAT CUSHION ASSEMBLY (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Detach the 2 front hooks of the seat cushion from the vehicle body.

atten3.ptxt: Follow the instructions below carefully as the cushion frame can be deformed easily.

s3.ptxt: Choose a hook to detach first. Place your hands near the hook as shown in the illustration. Then lift the seat cushion to detach the hook.
Repeat the step above for the other hook.

s2.ptxt: Remove the seat cushion.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR FLOOR SERVICE HOLE COVER

s2.ptxt: Remove the rear floor service hole cover.
Disconnect the fuel pump connector.

s-1.ptxt: SEPARATE FUEL PUMP TUBE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the tube joint clip, and pull out the fuel pump tube.

item.ptxt: Check that there is no dirt or other foreign objects around the connector before this connecting it. Clean the connector if necessary.
It is necessary to prevent mud or dirt from entering the quick connector. If mud or dirt gets in the connector, the O-rings may not seal properly.
Only this connect the quick connector by hand.
Do not bend, kink or twist the nylon tubes.
Protect the connector by covering it with a plastic bag.
If the pipe and connector are stuck, carefully try wiggling or pushing and pulling on the connector to release it. Pull the connector off carefully.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL TANK VENT TUBE SET PLATE

s2.ptxt: Remove the 8 bolts and set plate.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL SUCTION TUBE ASSEMBLY WITH PUMP AND GAUGE

s2.ptxt: Pull out the fuel suction tube from the fuel tank.

item.ptxt: Do not damage the fuel pump filter.
Be careful that the arm of the sender gauge does not bend.

s2.ptxt: Remove the gasket from the fuel suction tube.

s-1.ptxt: DRAIN FUEL
REMOVE CENTER EXHAUST PIPE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts, 2 nuts and center exhaust pipe assembly.
Remove the gasket from the front exhaust pipe.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 2 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the set bolt and set nut of the No. 2 parking brake cable assembly.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 3 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the set bolt and set nut of the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR STABILIZER BAR NO. 1 BRACKET

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 bolts and 2 rear stabilizer bar No. 1 brackets.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL TANK PROTECTOR LOWER CENTER

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 bolts and fuel tank protector (Except SE Grade).
Remove the 4 bolts and 2 clips (for SE Grade).
Remove the fuel tank protector (for SE Grade).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL TANK ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the fuel pump tube.

s3.ptxt: Pinch the tab of the retainer to disengage the lock claws and pull it down as shown in the illustration.
Pull out the fuel tank main tube.

item.ptxt: Check that there is no dirt or other foreign objects around the connector before disconnecting it. Clean the connector if necessary.
It is necessary to prevent mud or dirt from entering the quick connector. If mud or dirt gets in the connector, the O-rings may not seal properly.
Only disconnect the quick connector by hand.
Do not bend, kink or twist the nylon tubes. Protect the connector by covering it with a plastic bag.
If the pipe and connector are stuck, carefully try wiggling or pushing and pulling the connector to release it. Pull the connector off the pipe carefully.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the No. 1 fuel tube.

s3.ptxt: Pinch the tube connector and then pull out the No. 1 fuel tube.

item.ptxt: Check that there is no dirt or other foreign objects around the connector before disconnecting it. Clean the connector if necessary.
It is necessary to prevent mud or dirt from entering the quick connector. If mud or dirt gets in the connector, the O-rings may not seal properly.
Only disconnect the quick connector by hand.
Do not bend, kink or twist the nylon tubes. Protect the connector by covering it with a plastic bag.
If the pipe and connector are stuck, carefully try wiggling or pushing and pulling the connector to release it. Pull the connector off the pipe carefully.

s2.ptxt: Place a transmission jack under the fuel tank assembly.
Remove the 2 set bolts of the fuel tank bands. (*1)
Remove the hose clamp and disconnect the fuel tank to filter pipe hose. (*2)
Slightly lower the transmission jack.
Disconnect the fuel tank vent hose from the charcoal canister. (*3)

s3.ptxt: Push the connector deep into the charcoal canister to release the locking pin.

s2.ptxt: Remove the fuel tank.
Remove the 2 pins and 2 fuel tank bands as shown in the illustration.
Remove the 4 clip nuts (Except SE Grade).
Remove the 4 clip nuts (for SE Grade).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL TANK VENT HOSE

s2.ptxt: Push the connector deep into the charcoal canister to release the locking pin.
Pinch portion A.
Pull out the connector.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL PUMP TUBE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the fuel pump tube from the fuel main tube support.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FUEL MAIN TUBE SUPPORT

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and tube support.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 FUEL TANK CUSHION

s2.ptxt: Remove the 7 No. 2 fuel tank cushions.
